strategy: Hit with Storm — he takes +10% from it — and never attack into his 60% Myth resist.
Do not Feint him. Any Feint triggers Cleanse Ward that strips all traps off him. Stack blades instead of traps.
Grendelynn casts a 75% Intercept Shadow Sentinel on King Borr at the start, so single-target hits mostly land on the minion. Use an AoE to damage the boss directly.
Do not kill Grendelynn first — King Borr heals her to 100% and she recasts the Sentinel. Focus the boss; when he dies she is defeated too (Avenge Me).
Blade up and drop one boosted Storm AoE to finish King Borr through the intercept.
cheatNotes: Grendelynn puts a 75% Intercept Sentinel on King Borr, so most of a single-target hit is soaked by the minion. Use AoEs to hit the boss directly.
Feinting him backfires: he Cleanse Wards off all your traps. Skip traps and use blades.
If you kill Grendelynn before the boss, he heals her to full and she re-shields him. Do not bother killing the minion.
Join on time or the latecomer eats a Medusa (865).
Killing King Borr defeats Grendelynn automatically, so you never need to kill the minion yourself.
